abseqr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Analysing Nanobody CDR3 Sequences with abseqr

Extract CDR3 regions from antibody or nanobody sequences

Source: R/extract_cdr3.R
extract_cdr3.Rd

Identifies and extracts CDR3 regions using conserved flanking motifs. For nanobodies and VH domains the CDR3 is bounded by a conserved cysteine (C) on the N-terminal side and a conserved tryptophan (W) or phenylalanine (F) on the C-terminal side, following the Kabat/IMGT convention.

extract_cdr3(
  sequences,
  c_anchor = "C",
  w_anchor = "WG.G",
  min_length = 5L,
  max_length = 30L
)

Arguments

sequences

A character vector of amino acid sequences, or a tibble as returned by read_fasta() (must contain a sequence column).

c_anchor

Character. Regex for the conserved N-terminal residue preceding CDR3. Default "C".

w_anchor

Character. Regex for the conserved C-terminal motif following CDR3. Default "WG.G" (matches WGQG, WGAG, etc.).

min_length

Minimum CDR3 length to keep. Default 5L.

max_length

Maximum CDR3 length to keep. Default 30L.

Value

If sequences is a tibble, returns the same tibble with two added columns:

cdr3

Character. Extracted CDR3 sequence, or NA if not found.

cdr3_length

Integer. Length of CDR3, or NA.

If sequences is a character vector, returns a tibble with columns sequence, cdr3, and cdr3_length.

Details

The default anchor pattern C ... WGxG is the canonical nanobody CDR3 boundary. Use read_fasta() to load sequences first.

Examples

fasta_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_nanobodies.fasta",
                           package = "abseqr")
seqs <- read_fasta(fasta_file)
extract_cdr3(seqs)
#> # A tibble: 20 × 5
#>    header                      sequence                 length cdr3  cdr3_length
#>    <chr>                       <chr>                     <int> <chr>       <int>
#>  1 Nb_001|VHH|anti-lysozyme    QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AAGD…          18
#>  2 Nb_002|VHH|anti-GFP         EVQLVESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    119 ARDL…          12
#>  3 Nb_003|VHH|anti-HER2        QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    119 AAKD…          13
#>  4 Nb_004|VHH|anti-EGFR        EVQLLESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    122 ARDR…          15
#>  5 Nb_005|VHH|anti-TNF         QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARD…          18
#>  6 Nb_006|VHH|anti-VEGF        EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARG…          18
#>  7 Nb_007|VHH|anti-IL6         QVQLVESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    122 ARDH…          15
#>  8 Nb_008|VHH|anti-PD1         EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AATD…          18
#>  9 Nb_009|VHH|anti-CD38        QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    122 AAKD…          15
#> 10 Nb_010|VHH|anti-albumin     EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARD…          18
#> 11 Nb_011|VHH|anti-lysozyme-v2 QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AAGE…          18
#> 12 Nb_012|VHH|anti-fibrin      EVQLVESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    122 ARDR…          15
#> 13 Nb_013|VHH|anti-HER2-v2     QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    119 AAKD…          13
#> 14 Nb_014|VHH|anti-insulin     EVQLVESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    122 ARDR…          15
#> 15 Nb_015|VHH|anti-CTLA4       QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARD…          18
#> 16 Nb_016|VHH|anti-VEGF-v2     EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARG…          18
#> 17 Nb_017|VHH|anti-IL17        QVQLVESGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCA…    122 ARDH…          15
#> 18 Nb_018|VHH|anti-RSV         EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AATD…          18
#> 19 Nb_019|VHH|anti-influenza   QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    122 AAKD…          15
#> 20 Nb_020|VHH|anti-HIV         EVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCA…    125 AARD…          18

# Works on a plain character vector too
extract_cdr3(c("QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCAASGRTFSSYAMGWFRQAPGKEREFVAAISWSGGSTYYADSVKGRFTISRDNAKNTVYLQMNSLKPEDTAVYYCAAGDYYCSSTSCPIWYDYWGQGTQVTVSS"))
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   sequence                                                     cdr3  cdr3_length
#>   <chr>                                                        <chr>       <int>
#> 1 QVQLVESGGGLVQAGGSLRLSCAASGRTFSSYAMGWFRQAPGKEREFVAAISWSGGSTY… AAGD…          18
